documents.content:
The Auth Service is a core component of the Acme Labs platform, owned by the Platform team. It provides handles authentication and authorization.

Key dependencies: Payment Service, Notification Service.

Technology stack: TypeScript, GraphQL.

The service handles approximately 50k requests per minute in production. Leo Fernandez designed the current architecture during the Q3 rewrite. Primary maintainers: Frank Okafor, Nate Cooper.
documents.title: Auth Service architecture overview

documents.content:
The Notification Service is a core component of the Acme Labs platform, owned by the Platform team. It provides sends emails, push notifications, and webhooks.

Key dependencies: Analytics Ingestion, Auth Service.

Technology stack: PostgreSQL, Kubernetes.

The service handles approximately 50k requests per minute in production. Kate Johansson designed the current architecture during the Q3 rewrite. Primary maintainers: James O'Brien, Alice Chen.
documents.title: Notification Service architecture overview

documents.content:
The Search API is a core component of the Acme Labs platform, owned by the Engineering team. It provides full-text and semantic search across the platform.

Key dependencies: Analytics Ingestion, Notification Service.

Technology stack: Redis, Apache Spark.

The service handles approximately 50k requests per minute in production. Carol Washington designed the current architecture during the Q3 rewrite. Primary maintainers: Bob Martinez, Alice Chen.
documents.title: Search API architecture overview

documents.content:
The Analytics Ingestion is a core component of the Acme Labs platform, owned by the Data team. It provides collects and processes analytics events.

Key dependencies: Search API, Notification Service.

Technology stack: TypeScript, Apache Spark.

The service handles approximately 50k requests per minute in production. David Kim designed the current architecture during the Q3 rewrite. Primary maintainers: Frank Okafor, Frank Okafor.
documents.title: Analytics Ingestion architecture overview

documents.content:
The Notification Service is a core component of the Acme Labs platform, owned by the Platform team. It provides sends emails, push notifications, and webhooks.

Key dependencies: Payment Service, Analytics Ingestion.

Technology stack: Terraform, Kafka.

The service handles approximately 50k requests per minute in production. Kate Johansson designed the current architecture during the Q3 rewrite. Primary maintainers: Leo Fernandez, James O'Brien.
documents.title: Notification Service architecture overview

documents.content:
The Notification Service is a core component of the Acme Labs platform, owned by the Engineering team. It provides sends emails, push notifications, and webhooks.

Key dependencies: Analytics Ingestion, Payment Service.

Technology stack: Python, Elasticsearch.

The service handles approximately 50k requests per minute in production. Maya Singh designed the current architecture during the Q3 rewrite. Primary maintainers: Alice Chen, David Kim.
documents.title: Notification Service architecture overview

documents.content:
The Payment Service is a core component of the Acme Labs platform, owned by the Engineering team. It provides processes payments and manages subscriptions.

Key dependencies: Search API, Analytics Ingestion.

Technology stack: Kafka, Terraform.

The service handles approximately 50k requests per minute in production. Alice Chen designed the current architecture during the Q3 rewrite. Primary maintainers: Bob Martinez, Iris Nakamura.
documents.title: Payment Service architecture overview

documents.title: Payment Service architecture overview

documents.content:
This document describes the technical design for Billing Migration.

Goals: improve Auth Service performance by 3x while maintaining backwards compatibility.

Approach: introduce a caching layer using Python between the API gateway and Payment Service. Alice Chen will implement the cache invalidation strategy. Hassan Ali will update the client SDKs.

Risks: cache staleness could cause data inconsistency. Mitigation: TTL-based expiry with event-driven invalidation from Analytics Ingestion.
documents.title: Billing Migration technical design

documents.content:
This document describes the technical design for Data Lake Consolidation.

Goals: improve Auth Service performance by 3x while maintaining backwards compatibility.

Approach: introduce a caching layer using TypeScript between the API gateway and Search API. David Kim will implement the cache invalidation strategy. Frank Okafor will update the client SDKs.

Risks: cache staleness could cause data inconsistency. Mitigation: TTL-based expiry with event-driven invalidation from Notification Service.
documents.title: Data Lake Consolidation technical design

documents.content:
This document describes the technical design for Data Lake Consolidation.

Goals: improve Search API performance by 3x while maintaining backwards compatibility.

Approach: introduce a caching layer using TypeScript between the API gateway and Analytics Ingestion. David Kim will implement the cache invalidation strategy. Elena Petrov will update the client SDKs.

Risks: cache staleness could cause data inconsistency. Mitigation: TTL-based expiry with event-driven invalidation from Auth Service.
documents.title: Data Lake Consolidation technical design

documents.content:
This document describes the technical design for Data Lake Consolidation.

Goals: improve Search API performance by 3x while maintaining backwards compatibility.

Approach: introduce a caching layer using Elasticsearch between the API gateway and Analytics Ingestion. Elena Petrov will implement the cache invalidation strategy. Frank Okafor will update the client SDKs.

Risks: cache staleness could cause data inconsistency. Mitigation: TTL-based expiry with event-driven invalidation from Auth Service.

This document describes the technical design for Auth Overhaul.

Goals: improve Auth Service performance by 3x while maintaining backwards compatibility.

Approach: introduce a caching layer using GraphQL between the API gateway and Notification Service. Carol Washington will implement the cache invalidation strategy. Bob Martinez will update the client SDKs.

Risks: cache staleness could cause data inconsistency. Mitigation: TTL-based expiry with event-driven invalidation from Search API.

documents.content:
This document describes the technical design for Infrastructure Modernization.

Goals: improve Search API performance by 3x while maintaining backwards compatibility.

Approach: introduce a caching layer using Kafka between the API gateway and Notification Service. Leo Fernandez will implement the cache invalidation strategy. Nate Cooper will update the client SDKs.

Risks: cache staleness could cause data inconsistency. Mitigation: TTL-based expiry with event-driven invalidation from Auth Service.
documents.title: Infrastructure Modernization technical design

documents.content:
This document describes the technical design for Auth Overhaul.

Goals: improve Auth Service performance by 3x while maintaining backwards compatibility.

Approach: introduce a caching layer using Redis between the API gateway and Notification Service. Carol Washington will implement the cache invalidation strategy. Maya Singh will update the client SDKs.

Risks: cache staleness could cause data inconsistency. Mitigation: TTL-based expiry with event-driven invalidation from Analytics Ingestion.
documents.title: Auth Overhaul technical design

documents.content:
This document describes the technical design for ML Pipeline.

Goals: improve Analytics Ingestion performance by 3x while maintaining backwards compatibility.

Approach: introduce a caching layer using Terraform between the API gateway and Auth Service. Elena Petrov will implement the cache invalidation strategy. Olivia Reeves will update the client SDKs.

Risks: cache staleness could cause data inconsistency. Mitigation: TTL-based expiry with event-driven invalidation from Notification Service.

documents.content:
This document describes the technical design for Mobile App v2.

Goals: improve Auth Service performance by 3x while maintaining backwards compatibility.

Approach: introduce a caching layer using Kubernetes between the API gateway and Search API. Iris Nakamura will implement the cache invalidation strategy. Hassan Ali will update the client SDKs.

Risks: cache staleness could cause data inconsistency. Mitigation: TTL-based expiry with event-driven invalidation from Notification Service.

This document describes the technical design for Infrastructure Modernization.

Goals: improve Payment Service performance by 3x while maintaining backwards compatibility.

Approach: introduce a caching layer using Python between the API gateway and Analytics Ingestion. Leo Fernandez will implement the cache invalidation strategy. Kate Johansson will update the client SDKs.

Risks: cache staleness could cause data inconsistency. Mitigation: TTL-based expiry with event-driven invalidation from Notification Service.
documents.title: Infrastructure Modernization technical design

documents.content:
The data ingestion pipeline for ML Pipeline processes events from Analytics Ingestion and Notification Service. Designed by Elena Petrov, maintained by Olivia Reeves.

Flow: events land in the message queue, are transformed by the TypeScript workers, validated against the schema registry, and written to the Kafka data store.

Current throughput: 10k events/sec. Scaling plan: partition by tenant ID. Hassan Ali is researching Python as an alternative processing engine.
documents.title: Data pipeline: ML Pipeline ingestion flow

documents.content:
The data ingestion pipeline for Search Redesign processes events from Auth Service and Analytics Ingestion. Designed by Kate Johansson, maintained by Leo Fernandez.

Flow: events land in the message queue, are transformed by the Terraform workers, validated against the schema registry, and written to the Kubernetes data store.

Current throughput: 10k events/sec. Scaling plan: partition by tenant ID. Nate Cooper is researching GraphQL as an alternative processing engine.
documents.title: Data pipeline: Search Redesign ingestion flow

Current throughput: 10k events/sec. Scaling plan: partition by tenant ID. Grace Liu is researching TypeScript as an alternative processing engine.

The data ingestion pipeline for Billing Migration processes events from Auth Service and Search API. Designed by Grace Liu, maintained by Hassan Ali.

Current throughput: 10k events/sec. Scaling plan: partition by tenant ID. Iris Nakamura is researching PostgreSQL as an alternative processing engine.

documents.content:
Severity: P1
Duration: 47 minutes
Impact: all Billing Migration users affected

Root cause: a misconfigured deployment of Auth Service caused a cascading failure in Analytics Ingestion. The Apache Spark connection pool was exhausted, which blocked all downstream requests.

Detection: Kate Johansson noticed elevated error rates in the monitoring dashboard at 14:23 UTC. James O'Brien was paged and confirmed the issue.

Resolution: Olivia Reeves rolled back the deployment. James O'Brien applied a hotfix to the connection pool configuration.

Action items: add connection pool health checks, implement circuit breaker between Auth Service and Analytics Ingestion.
documents.title: Incident: Auth Service outage on 2025-05-17

documents.content:

Detection: Elena Petrov noticed elevated error rates in the monitoring dashboard at 14:23 UTC. Nate Cooper was paged and confirmed the issue.

Resolution: Leo Fernandez rolled back the deployment. Nate Cooper applied a hotfix to the connection pool configuration.

Action items: add connection pool health checks, implement circuit breaker between Auth Service and Search API.

documents.title: Incident: Analytics Ingestion outage on 2025-01-17

documents.content:
Severity: P2
Duration: 2 hours
Impact: Analytics Dashboard experienced 3x latency increase

Root cause: Alice Chen's query optimization for Analytics Dashboard introduced a full table scan on the Kubernetes index. The Analytics Ingestion CPU usage spiked to 95%.

Detection: automated alerting triggered. David Kim investigated.

Resolution: reverted the query change. Olivia Reeves from Data helped design a proper index strategy.

Action items: add query plan review to PR checklist for Analytics Ingestion changes.
documents.title: Incident: Analytics Ingestion degraded performance

documents.content:
Status: Approved
Decision maker: David Kim
Date: 2026-01-04

Context: the current GraphQL stack for Data Lake Consolidation is reaching scaling limits. Olivia Reeves benchmarked alternatives and Apache Spark showed 4x throughput improvement.

Decision: migrate Data Lake Consolidation to Apache Spark over Q2. Frank Okafor will lead the migration. The Data team will provide support.

Tradeoffs: higher operational complexity, team needs to learn Apache Spark. Mitigated by: Olivia Reeves already has experience, will run internal training sessions.
documents.title: Decision: migrate Data Lake Consolidation to Apache Spark

Tradeoffs: higher operational complexity, team needs to learn Redis. Mitigated by: Frank Okafor already has experience, will run internal training sessions.

Context: Analytics Ingestion was originally built by Engineering but is now primarily used by Analytics Dashboard, which is owned by Data. Alice Chen has been the de facto maintainer despite being on Engineering.

Tradeoffs: Data takes on additional operational load. Mitigated by: Alice Chen remains available for escalations.

Context: Search API was originally built by Data but is now primarily used by Search Redesign, which is owned by Engineering. Alice Chen has been the de facto maintainer despite being on Data.

Decision: transfer Search API ownership to Engineering. Alice Chen will be the new primary maintainer. Alice Chen will provide a 2-week knowledge transfer.

Tradeoffs: Engineering takes on additional operational load. Mitigated by: Alice Chen remains available for escalations.

The Data team approved this at the architecture review on 2025-09-02.
